artifacts.fmrimod_expresses_better: The F contrast intent, condition-minus-condition t hypothesis, drift terms, confounds, and design-column provenance are represented as typed fmrimod objects; the realised t/F matrices are only the Nilearn reference adapter.
The crossed task-by-valence design is authored as typed factors and resolved through design-column metadata instead of manual pseudo-trial-type and contrast-index bookkeeping.

artifacts.fmrimod_path: benchmarks/parity/tier_a_factorial_3x3_parametric/workflow.py::fmrimod_pipeline: fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm -> precomputed factorial and parametric contrast vectors
artifacts.fmrimod_expresses_better: The nine-cell factorial and per-cell parametric modulator remain inspectable typed design terms, including NaN-modulator recovery and rank diagnostics, instead of becoming duplicated event labels. The executable fmrimod contrast calls still consume precomputed vectors/matrices, so this is not yet a typed contrast-authoring UX receipt.
ux_blockers: difficulty, emotion, interaction, and parametric omnibus hypotheses still pass inputs.c_* arrays to fit.contrast
needs authored semantic contrast sugar for factorial trends and parametric terms

artifacts.fmrimod_expresses_better: The 2x2 factorial main effects (sentence_effect, speaker_effect) resolve as typed column_contrast patterns against the realised design rather than hand-built weight vectors, and the per-run designs flow through RealizedDesign(source='nilearn') so the Nilearn provenance survives the fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm -> combine_runs seam. This is an interop/provenance receipt, not native design-authoring UX.
ux_blockers: FIAC still consumes Nilearn-realized design matrices instead of authoring the event model natively in fmrimod

artifacts.fmrimod_path: benchmarks/parity/tier_a_fir_unconstrained_hrf/workflow.py::fmrimod_pipeline: fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm -> typed FIR semantic contrast + OmnibusContrast
artifacts.fmrimod_expresses_better: The FIR lag expansion, per-lag t hypothesis, and omnibus hypothesis are carried as typed basis and contrast intent rather than hand-enumerated lag column indices.

The externally realized Nilearn design enters as a typed RealizedDesign with DesignColumns and source provenance, so the public fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm seam can fit and contrast it without raw matrix helpers. This is an interop/provenance receipt, not native localizer design-authoring UX.
ux_blockers: localizer still fits Nilearn's realized design instead of authoring the first-level design natively in fmrimod

artifacts.fmrimod_expresses_better: The rank-deficient nuisance design produces explicit aliased-column diagnostics and condition reports while preserving identifiable contrast parity through authored task t/F contrasts.

replacement_target.description: The exact concatenated-denominator oracle remains as a numerical canary beneath the public-seam multirun row.
replacement_target.blocking_api_gap: The canary intentionally uses private matrix kernels to match Nilearn's single concatenated-design denominator exactly.

artifacts.fmrimod_path: benchmarks/parity/tier_a_multirun_concat/workflow.py::public_seam_pipeline: matrix_dataset -> RealizedDesign(source='fmrimod') -> fmri_lm -> public vector contrasts
artifacts.fmrimod_expresses_better: The public seam carries a fmrimod-realised concatenated design through RealizedDesign(source='fmrimod') and proves the matrix/result path can fit cross-run contrasts without private kernels. The executable public-seam contrast calls still use precomputed vectors, so this is a matrix-seam/provenance receipt rather than a full native authoring UX receipt.
ux_blockers: public_seam_pipeline lifts inputs.design through RealizedDesign instead of authoring the concatenated multi-run spec at the call site
cross-run hypotheses still pass inputs.c_* arrays to fit.contrast

artifacts.fmrimod_path: benchmarks/parity/tier_a_parametric_modulation/workflow.py::fmrimod_pipeline: fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm -> typed parametric design -> condition(...) and modulator(...).within(...).slope(...) sugar for t contrasts; F omnibus still consumes precomputed matrix (v2 deferral)
artifacts.fmrimod_expresses_better: Main effects and continuous modulators are authored in one typed HRF term and resolved by design metadata instead of duplicated event rows or convolver-order guesses. The t-contrast call sites for the main A-B and the parametric slope difference now use the typed condition(...) and modulator(...).within(...).slope(...) sugar so user code no longer names internal term:modulator column shapes. The F-omnibus over the two parametric columns still consumes a precomputed matrix because the F-contrast spelling is deferred to v2 per docs/contracts/parametric_contrast_sugar_v1.md.
ux_blockers: F-omnibus contrast over parametric columns still passes inputs.c_param_F to fit.contrast; F-contrast sugar (modulator(...).within(...).slopes(...) or .omnibus(...)) is deferred to v2 per docs/contracts/parametric_contrast_sugar_v1.md

artifacts.fmrimod_path: benchmarks/parity/tier_a_spm_auditory/workflow.py::fmrimod_pipeline: fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm -> contrast via SemanticContrast -> contrast_receipt
artifacts.fmrimod_expresses_better: The user-facing path keeps HRF normalization, dataset construction, fitting, and contrast evaluation as typed inspectable objects instead of a stateful FirstLevelModel call.

artifacts.fmrimod_path: benchmarks/parity/tier_a_spm_derivative_basis/workflow.py::fmrimod_pipeline: fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm(hrf(basis='spmg2', norm='spm')) -> contrast via SemanticContrast(basis_ix=1) -> contrast_receipt
artifacts.fmrimod_expresses_better: The informed-basis-set expansion (canonical + temporal derivative) is selected by a typed HRF kind (basis='spmg2') and the canonical-side contrast is pinned by SemanticContrast(condition(..., basis_ix=1)) — multi-basis contrast intent is recorded in the contrast receipt rather than implied by column position.

artifacts.fmrimod_path: benchmarks/parity/tier_b_fitlins_bids/workflow.py::translated_pipeline: BIDS JSON -> translate_run_node -> fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm -> typed BIDS contrasts via StatsModelTranslation.fit -> compute_contrasts
artifacts.fmrimod_expresses_better: BIDS Stats Model JSON flows into the public fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm -> contrast seam as typed Spec + StatsModelContrast records; the JSON node is the single source of truth, instead of FitLins's TSV-vector + statmap-file hand-off. Expressible BIDS t/F contrasts lower through fmrimod semantic or omnibus contrast objects, while unsupported shapes remain on an explicitly named vector bridge fallback.
The group path carries subject maps as a GroupDataset and authors both one-sample and age-regression models with group_model(...) typed intent before dispatching to the native voxelwise OLS reducer.

replacement_target.description: Promote AR(p) and robust refit into a public dataset/model workflow receipt instead of a low-level residual canary.
replacement_target.blocking_api_gap: Public typed AR/robust model configuration and reportable contrast output are not yet wired through fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm.

artifacts.reference_path: benchmarks/parity/tier_d_showcase/workflow.py::run_lss_public_seam_showcase ground-truth recovery oracle and tier_d_lss_trialwise_oracle matrix exactness canary
artifacts.fmrimod_expresses_better: The public-seam wrapper takes only the dataset and a string spec ('trialwise()' or 'trialwise(basis=...)' ) and returns a typed SingleTrialResult carrying per-trial betas plus recovered trial_labels; the call site never reaches into the realised design matrix and never builds a per-trial weight vector by hand. The matrix oracle (tier_d_lss_trialwise_oracle) survives as a numerical canary beneath this public-seam row. The remaining ergonomic gap is that the single-trial model itself is still authored as a string spec.
ux_blockers: estimate_single_trial_from_dataset currently takes 'trialwise()' / 'trialwise(basis=...)' string specs instead of a typed trialwise value

replacement_target.description: Matrix LSS exactness check stays as the oracle beneath the public-seam tier_d_lss_trialwise row; promoting this canary itself is not in scope.
replacement_target.blocking_api_gap: The matrix-first call site exists for algebraic drift detection; the public-seam flagship is the sibling tier_d_lss_trialwise row pointing at run_lss_public_seam_showcase.

artifacts.reference_path: benchmarks/parity/tier_d_showcase/workflow.py::_lss_reference and exact GLM helpers plus tier_group_semantic_survival oracle
artifacts.fmrimod_path: benchmarks/parity/tier_d_showcase/workflow.py::render: fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm -> OmnibusContrast -> ContrastResult.explain -> GroupDataset -> group_model -> ols_voxelwise
artifacts.fmrimod_expresses_better: The showcase report carries only public-seam evidence: LSS rows through dataset/spec wrappers, semantic survival of typed contrast intent into a GroupDataset, typed group_model authoring, recorded timing, caveats, and win-axis fields instead of a loose numeric demo table. Low-level Tier D canaries are classified as separate numerical-canary artifacts. The aggregate still inherits the LSS string-spec UX gap from its component rows.

replacement_target.description: Promote sketched GLM into a public typed engine option with an end-to-end dataset/spec/contrast receipt.
replacement_target.blocking_api_gap: Low-rank engine selection is still demonstrated as a matrix-level capability rather than a typed fmri_lm engine option.

replacement_target.description: Promote any observed fmrimod boundary that survives review into a public-seam caveat or solver fix, and convert the survivable case to fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm once typed stress-design construction exists.

artifacts.reference_path: synthetic truth: BIDS-authored task effects with behavior-moderated parametric slope differences
artifacts.fmrimod_path: benchmarks/parity/tier_e_bids_parametric_group_followthrough/workflow.py::run_benchmark: BIDS JSON -> translate_run_node -> fmri_dataset -> StatsModelContrast.apply -> modulator('rt_z').within('trial_type') -> group_dataset_from_contrasts -> group_model('behavior') -> ols_voxelwise
artifacts.fmrimod_expresses_better: A compact BIDS Stats Model JSON lowers to typed first-level fitting, BIDS task contrasts lower to semantic contrast objects, and the parametric RT slope is authored as modulator('rt_z').within('trial_type').slope('word') - modulator('rt_z').within('trial_type').slope('pseudoword') with no user-authored raw contrast vector. First-level ContrastResult objects flow into group modeling through group_dataset_from_contrasts, so the receipt no longer requires manual subject/feature/beta/se row packing.

The RT modulator, centering column, and task levels remain inspectable through typed fmrimod design objects; the Nilearn reference sees the same GLM only after the design has already been realized as anonymous columns. The main-effect/slope contrasts still come from raw vectors, so typed contrast authoring remains open.
ux_blockers: fmrimod_probe calls _contrasts(...) to build raw main/slope vectors before fit.contrast; parametric semantic contrast sugar is tracked by bd-01KRM5FRQP16S05T3DA57DQY31 and bd-01KRM6BY25444C5ZTHRGDBHVFN

replacement_target.description: Promote surviving reparameterization-invariance probes into a public-seam fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm stress receipt once typed design transforms and contrast remapping are available.
replacement_target.blocking_api_gap: no typed design transforms / contrast remapping for stress probes; fast_preproject + fast_lm_matrix bypass fmri_lm.

artifacts.fmrimod_expresses_better: Censoring lives on the dataset and A-minus-B resolves through declared semantic contrast provenance; the Nilearn oracle matches only when handed the realized row-subset design explicitly, while the compressed-timebase variant silently changes the modeled hypothesis.

artifacts.fmrimod_path: benchmarks/parity/tier_e_semantic_contrast_alignment/workflow.py::fmrimod_probe + fmrimod_authored_probe: fit_glm_from_matrix(named DataFrame / source DesignColumns) -> column_contrast and condition('gain') - condition('loss') -> ContrastResult
artifacts.fmrimod_expresses_better: The gain-minus-loss hypothesis is resolved by named column intent and by an authored condition contrast against declared DesignColumns; ordinary event designs now carry that authored condition contrast through fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm. The exact Nilearn parity trap remains a matrix-first fixture because Nilearn needs a freshly rebuilt numeric vector and a reused positional vector silently targets the wrong coefficients.

replacement_target.description: Promote the exact semantic contrast-alignment stress to the canonical authored-model path once this benchmark fixture is expressed as events/spec rather than a matrix-first source DesignColumns fixture.
replacement_target.blocking_api_gap: Authored condition contrasts now resolve through fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm for ordinary event designs; the remaining gap is porting this exact column-permutation stress to an event/spec fixture with a matched Nilearn oracle.

artifacts.fmrimod_path: benchmarks/parity/tier_group_semantic_survival/workflow.py::run_demo: fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm -> OmnibusContrast -> ContrastResult.explain -> GroupDataset -> group_model -> ols_voxelwise
